info.name: Winoe AI - Backend QA Collection
info.description:
Comprehensive QA test collection for the Winoe AI backend API. Covers all endpoints across auth, trials, candidate sessions, tasks, submissions, winoe reports, admin operations, and GitHub webhooks.

**Setup:**
1. Import the 'Winoe AI - Backend QA' environment
2. Set base_url (default: http://localhost:8000)
3. For local testing with DEV_AUTH_BYPASS=1, set:
   - talent_partner_token = talent_partner:<talent_partner_email>
   - candidate_token = candidate:<candidate_email>
4. For production, use Auth0 JWT tokens
5. Set admin_api_key for admin endpoints

**Auth Patterns:**
- TalentPartner endpoints: Bearer token with talent_partner role
- Candidate endpoints: Bearer token with candidate role
- Task endpoints: Bearer token + x-candidate-session-id header
- Admin template endpoints: X-Admin-Key header
- Admin demo endpoints: Bearer token (demo admin allowlisted)

item.name: 00 - Health & Server
item.description: Server health and diagnostic endpoints. No authentication required.

item.name: 01 - Auth & Identity
item.description: Authentication and user identity endpoints. Uses Auth0 JWT (or DEV_AUTH_BYPASS shorthand tokens in local/test). Set talent_partner_token and candidate_token in the environment before running.

body.raw:
{
  "contentText": "# Design Document

## Overview
This document outlines the architecture for the feed rendering pipeline.

## Architecture
- Component-based design using React with TypeScript
- State management via Redux Toolkit
- API layer using RTK Query

## Data Flow
1. User scrolls feed
2. Intersection Observer triggers fetch
3. Virtualized list renders visible items
4. Optimistic updates for interactions

## Testing Strategy
- Unit tests for components
- Integration tests for data flow
- E2E tests for critical user journeys"
}

exec: pm.test("Status code is 201 or 400/404/409/422", function () { pm.expect([201, 400, 404, 409, 422]).to.include(pm.response.code); });
pm.test("Status code is 201 or 400/404/409/422", function () { pm.expect([201, 400, 404, 409, 422]).to.include(pm.response.code); });

body.raw:
{
  "contentText": "# Reflection

This trial challenged me to think about component architecture at scale. I learned that performance optimization requires careful consideration of rendering cycles and state management patterns.

## What I Would Do Differently
- Start with a more detailed component hierarchy
- Write tests earlier in the process
- Spend more time on accessibility

## Key Takeaways
- Virtual scrolling is essential for large lists
- Redux Toolkit simplifies state management significantly
- TypeScript catches many bugs at compile time"
}

exec: pm.test("Status code is 201 or 400/404/409/422", function () { pm.expect([201, 400, 404, 409, 422]).to.include(pm.response.code); });

item.name: 99 - Full E2E Happy Path Flow
item.description: Complete end-to-end flow from talent_partner login through winoe report retrieval. Run sequentially with Postman Collection Runner. Each step chains to the next via environment variables. PREREQUISITES: DEV_AUTH_BYPASS=1, WINOE_ENV=local, running server on port 8000.

}

body.raw:
{
  "contentText": "# Final Architecture Design

## Overview
This document describes the complete system architecture.

## Components
- API Gateway Layer
- Service Layer
- Repository Layer
- Database Layer

## Data Models
- User, Trial, Task, Submission

## Testing Strategy
- 99% code coverage target
- Integration tests with real database"
}
